title: Phenomenon 33
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 33: Unnatural Recklessness}}__NOTOC__ {{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Rubik+Iso%7CRubik+Iso');}}{{CSS|content=@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Charm%7CCharm');}} == Description == '''Phemonenon 33''', commonly known as "'''Unnatural Recklessness'''", is a mental disorder affecting wanderers throughout [[The Backrooms]]. When a person shows signs of the severe lack of fear, to the point where they are ignorant of the dangers in front of them, they are called the victims of '''Unnatural Recklessness'''. This phenomenon seems insignificant yet extremely dangerous if the victim contracts it during explorations or entity sightings. Despite its prolonged existence, no researchers have developed a cure for it, nor have they proven the existence of one. == Causes == Following the discovery of this phenomenon, people often speculated it to be caused by pathogens due to its contagious nature. However, no microorganisms or viruses have been identified as the reason for the peculiar behavior of those infected. A month after the discovery, a scientist found that most infected people had near-death experiences within a week, so he proposed a hypothesis related to it. It was then popularized and proven true. This phenomenon is also thought to be contagious through everyday actions and attitudes of the infected towards other people, even though if the other person had not had near-death experiences at all. == Symptoms == === 5-10 minutes === Unlike [https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Post-traumatic_stress_disorder PTSD], patients of '''Unnatural Recklessness''' seems to be desensitized towards traumatic experiences. They would start to become more emotionless towards daily events, and self-isolate themselves from other people. === 10-30 minutes === The victim shows a clear sign of annoyance towards everything in their sight. They refused to talk to other people most of the time, but would talk reluctantly. Sometimes, they might attack whoever slightly inconvenienced them. The patient became impulsive and had no acknowledgement or understanding of any danger nearby. === Beyond 30 minutes === After this point, the infected became highly aggressive and ignorant, their new behaviors included: *Approaching dangerous or lethal entities in an attempt to befriend them, not understanding the consequences; *Committing dangerous acts, potentially harming other people in the proccess; *Let loose all impulsive thoughts, became selfish and arrogant, etc. Although this phenomenon does not necessarily cause death to the infected or the others, the dangers of it are not negligible, and need to be stay aware of.
Dear Mr Collins

I am writing to you to announce I have finally found the potential cause of this recklessness behavior among our patients.

According to the test results, I have found most of our patients had survived some sort of entity attacks. It seemed that the traumatic experience had affected their brains, which makes them not aware of any dangers anymore. Hereby, I may hypothesize that this is the reason for it.

The biggest problem about this is finding a cure for our patients. I had tried to apply sedative pills, but it is no use. In fact, all medical procedures I had done cannot stop their actions without them passing out, which does not help them either. The only time when they seemed quieter is when I tried to talk to them patiently. It is when then I realized therapy is the only choice for them. It does not exactly cure them, but it does help calm them down and be conscious about their actions.

Hope you find my information useful for further researching purposes.

Yours Sincerely
Colleague
George S.
